description:
You’re a security engineer reviewing a credential-rotation log.  
    An archived text file already exists at

        /home/user/security/rotation.log

    1. Read the log and, using a *single* regular-expression filter (e.g., grep/egrep/awk/sed) plus a couple of quick text-processing commands (overall 3–4 commands in total), isolate only the **successful** rotation entries.  
       • A “successful” entry is an `INFO` line that literally contains the phrase  
         `rotated credential` (the file also contains `WARN` lines for failures; ignore them).  

    2. From every successful line, extract exactly the following three fields:  
       a) the ISO-8601 timestamp at the very start of the line  
       b) the old access-key ID (the token that begins with `AKIA` immediately after the words “credential”)  
       c) the new access-key ID (the token that begins with `AKIA` immediately after the word `to`)  

    3. Produce a neatly formatted report saved to

        /home/user/security/rotation_report.txt

       The report must contain *one line per successful rotation* with the fields in this precise order, separated by a comma and a single space:

           YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, OLD_KEY, NEW_KEY

       Example (spacing and punctuation must match exactly):

           2024-05-21T09:14:03Z, AKIAOLD123, AKIANEW321

       The automated test will compare the file byte-for-byte, so be precise—no trailing or leading spaces, no additional columns, blank lines, or headers.

    4. For quick human confirmation, also echo the exact contents of rotation_report.txt to STDOUT after creating it.

    NOTES/GUIDELINES  
    • Limit yourself to about 3–4 simple shell commands in total; pipeline usage is encouraged.  
    • Do **not** modify the original log file.  
    • Do not add extra commentary in the report file—only the data lines in the format above.

    Outcome expected by the grader  
    • File /home/user/security/rotation_report.txt exists and contains only the correctly extracted and formatted lines.  
    • The agent’s terminal output ends by printing those same lines.
truth:
Initial filesystem setup (already present when the task begins):

    Directory: /home/user/security
      – rotation.log  (permissions 0644)

    Contents of /home/user/security/rotation.log
    ----------------------------------------------------
    2024-05-21T09:14:03Z INFO User john rotated credential AKIAOLD123 to AKIANEW321 from IP 10.0.0.12
    2024-05-21T10:22:17Z WARN Rotation failed for credential AKIAOLD456 from IP 10.0.0.7
    2024-05-21T11:33:01Z INFO User alice rotated credential AKIAOLD789 to AKIANEW654 from IP 10.0.0.23
    2024-05-22T12:01:55Z INFO User bob rotated credential AKIAOLD000 to AKIANEW999 from IP 10.0.0.5
    2024-05-22T13:09:42Z WARN Rotation failed for credential AKIAOLD888 from IP 10.0.0.29
    2024-05-22T14:45:17Z INFO User carol rotated credential AKIAOLD222 to AKIANEW333 from IP 10.0.0.44
    ----------------------------------------------------

    Expected file to be created by the agent:

    /home/user/security/rotation_report.txt  (permissions 0644)

    Exact required contents of rotation_report.txt
    ----------------------------------------------------
    2024-05-21T09:14:03Z, AKIAOLD123, AKIANEW321
    2024-05-21T11:33:01Z, AKIAOLD789, AKIANEW654
    2024-05-22T12:01:55Z, AKIAOLD000, AKIANEW999
    2024-05-22T14:45:17Z, AKIAOLD222, AKIANEW333
    ----------------------------------------------------
